findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-003 — MCP cluster_resolve returns sanitized output for ALL FIVE store types (artifact, canonical, receipt, ledger, index)
legs_asserted: MCP cluster_resolve on artifact URI: storagePath undefined, content undefined, _sourceType='owner-truth', _contentPolicy contains DATA/instructions, non-sensitive fields present
legs_missing: MCP cluster_resolve on canonical URI returns sanitized entity
MCP cluster_resolve on receipt URI returns sanitized receipt
MCP cluster_resolve on ledger URI returns sanitized provenance event
MCP cluster_resolve on index URI returns sanitized index record
findings.evidence: test only invokes handleTool('cluster_resolve', { uri: artifact-uri }, sdk); no parallel block for the other 4 URI types. src/mcp/server.ts:541-545 only special-cases artifact + canonical; other 3 rely on SDK upstream sanitization. A future refactor that constructs the SDK without policies (or drops SDK-level ledger/index/receipt sanitization) would leak through MCP with no test catching it.

findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-003 — SDK.resolve ledger output is sanitized: actorId='[REDACTED]' AND detail={} AND _sourceType='audit-record'
legs_asserted: AT LEAST ONE of: _sourceType==='audit-record', actorId==='', actorId==='[REDACTED]', !detail, detail empty
legs_missing: All three conditions hold simultaneously (the test is a disjunction; a regression that emits only ONE sanitization marker passes)
findings.evidence: lines 273-280: `const isSanitized = ledgerObj._sourceType === 'audit-record' || ledgerObj.actorId === '' || ledgerObj.actorId === '[REDACTED]' || !ledgerObj.detail || Object.keys(ledgerObj.detail).length === 0; expect(isSanitized).toBe(true);` — the OR-chain accepts any one of 5 markers. Production at store-output-sanitizers.ts:82-87 always emits all three. A future change that emits only _sourceType but leaves actorId raw would silently pass.

findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-003 — SDK.resolve index output: metadata stripped AND _sourceType='derivative' AND _metadataPolicy present
legs_asserted: AT LEAST ONE of: _sourceType defined, !metadata, metadata empty
legs_missing: _sourceType pinned to 'derivative' specifically
_metadataPolicy field present with notice text
metadata explicitly UNDEFINED (production sanitizer removes it via destructuring); a regression that leaves metadata={} would pass
findings.evidence: lines 289-293: disjunction. Production at store-output-sanitizers.ts:53-57 emits _sourceType:'derivative' + _metadataPolicy + completely removes metadata. Same either-OK pattern.

findings.invariant: KERNEL-R2-001 — inspectCommand error type is IDENTICAL between existent and nonexistent commandIds for any denied principal, including denial at the second commandVerb-refined enforce stage
legs_asserted: denied-at-coarse-gate principal: existent and nonexistent commandIds both yield PolicyDeniedError
legs_missing: denied-at-commandVerb-refinement principal: when policy allows coarse `read_command` but denies a specific commandVerb, existent commandIds still yield PolicyDeniedError but nonexistent commandIds yield NotFoundError — the second-stage refinement re-introduces the oracle
findings.evidence: src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts:488-499 implements a 2-stage enforce: coarse pre-fetch + commandVerb-refined post-fetch. The fix comment at L482-487 acknowledges 'we propagate NotFoundError' when coarse passes. Test at wave-a3-kernel-regression.test.ts:38-43 uses roles:[] (denied at coarse). No test exercises a principal allowed at coarse but denied at refinement.

findings.invariant: STORES-R2-004 — TraceBuilder.eventToEdgeType for 'mutation_orphaned' returns the SPECIFIC edge type 'missing_provenance'
legs_missing: edge type IS the specific value 'missing_provenance' — a regression that changes the mapping to any other value (e.g., 'orphan_edge', 'mutation_failed', a typo, empty string) still passes the negative assertion
findings.evidence: test line 294: `expect(edgeType).not.toBe('entity_created_by');` — only assertion. src/provenance/trace-builder.ts:437 sets specific value 'missing_provenance' that downstream trace consumers will key off.

findings.invariant: KERNEL-R2-006 — redactProvenanceEvent handles ALL three rule types (provenance_actors, command_payload, receipt_details), with receipt_details wiping entire detail
legs_asserted: command_payload strip → payload/commandId/kind/entityId/name removed, verb preserved
legs_missing: receipt_details rule → detail object entirely emptied (the most aggressive code path at src/policy/redactor.ts:151-153 'Aggressive: drop the whole detail object'); no test exercises this rule type at all
findings.evidence: redactor.ts:147-153 has the receipt_details branch. Test file imports RedactionRule (line 25) but creates rules only with target='provenance_actors' (line 510) and target='command_payload' (line 542). No `target: 'receipt_details'` rule passed to redactProvenanceEvent. Removing redactor.ts:151-153 leaves the detail intact for callers with that rule active — no test catches.

findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-002 — buildSDKOptions REJECTS a DB_CLUSTER_POLICIES_FILE that resolves outside cwd via symlink
legs_asserted: When symlink can be created: buildSDKOptions throws AND error does not contain leak marker
legs_missing: When symlinkSync fails with EPERM/ENOTSUP (Windows without admin/Developer Mode): the test silently returns without asserting the invariant. On the 5080 Windows rig the invariant is never exercised — test passes trivially.
findings.evidence: lines 153-159: `} catch (err: any) { if (err.code === 'EPERM' || err.code === 'ENOTSUP') { console.warn(...); return; } throw err; }` — `return` exits without any expect() call. On Windows without Dev Mode, this test is no-op.

findings.invariant: KERNEL-R2-005 — link_evidence orphan event cites the affected entity id
legs_missing: the orphan event cites the entity id on subjectId OR detail.entityId (parity with TESTS-R2-001 leg 5 for createEntity at wave-a3-tests-regression.test.ts:161-171). A regression that records mutation_orphaned with empty subjectId would still pass.
findings.evidence: test lines 416-424 — no assertion on subjectId or detail.entityId. Production src/kernel/cluster-kernel.ts:368-373 passes input.entityId as the subject. createEntity test asserts citation; link_evidence test omits same assertion.

findings.invariant: KERNEL-R2-002 — verify() ignores ALL ledger-subject events as orphan candidates (command_approved, command_rejected, command_compensated, mutation_committed-with-targetStore=ledger)
legs_missing: command_compensated (compensateMutation full lifecycle) → healthy
mutation_committed events with targetStore='ledger' (e.g., link_evidence) → healthy. Re-audit-2 explicitly listed all 5 ledger-subject event types. 2 of 5 tested.
findings.evidence: test file has describe block 'KERNEL-R2-002' with only two it() blocks covering approved + rejected. compensateMutation and link_evidence's mutation_committed-with-ledger-subject are absent.

findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-006 — CLI commit --self-approve without --accept-soft-duty-bypass REFUSES AND DOES NOT perform the auto-walk (command remains in 'proposed' state)
legs_missing: After refusal, the command is still in 'proposed' state — no validate/approve/commit side effects happened. A regression that refuses on the final commit step but DID auto-walk validate→approve would print warning, exit non-zero, AND leave the command in 'approved' state — silent state corruption.
findings.evidence: test lines 413-416 only assert exit status + message. No call to db-cluster inspect or kernel.inspectCommand to verify command status. Production places the refusal check BEFORE the walk so the invariant holds — but a future refactor that reorders would break silently.

findings.invariant: STORES-R2-005 — LocalArtifactStore.ingest on tmp-write failure path EXECUTES the cleanup unlinkSync(tmpPath), leaving no .tmp file under contentDir
legs_asserted: finalPath does not exist as a partial file
legs_missing: The unlinkSync cleanup path actually executes — test setup with EISDIR triggers writeFileSync failure BEFORE any .tmp file is created; cleanup branch never runs. A regression that removes the try/catch+unlink cleanup at L111-120 leaves no observable artifact in this test setup.
The happy path tmp+rename atomicity is not asserted.
findings.evidence: test setup uses mkdirSync(tmpPath, ...) so writeFileSync throws EISDIR BEFORE creating .tmp. The catch block's unlinkSync runs on a directory and throws EPERM, swallowed. The 'no .tmp orphans' check trivially passes. Second test (L424-449) is source-text grep — comment mentioning .tmp would satisfy.

findings.invariant: SURFACE-R2-004 — INTERNAL_TRUSTED_PRINCIPAL warning fires IFF (policies non-empty AND principal undefined)
legs_asserted: policies + INTERNAL principal explicit → NO WARN
legs_missing: NO policies + NO principal → NO WARN (false-positive guard: a regression that fires the warning on the no-policy path would silently break the baseline-tests path)
findings.evidence: test has 3 it() blocks covering 3 'with policies' cases. cluster-sdk.ts:149-186 places the warning INSIDE if(policyConfigured). 4 combinations of (policies×principal) need 4 cases; 3 present.

findings.invariant: KERNEL-R2-006 — redactProvenanceEvent behaves consistently across all 4 RedactionRule behaviors (strip, mask, summarize, hash)
legs_asserted: strip behavior on detail → fields removed
legs_missing: mask behavior, summarize behavior, hash behavior
findings.evidence: fast-check property at L578-590 hard-codes behavior:'strip' for both rule types. The pre-fix bug was strip→'' vs mask→REDACTED; fix unified both. A regression that re-introduces behavior-dependent output is not probed.

findings.invariant: STORES-R2-003 — verify() reports degraded with no_orphaned_mutations as the SPECIFIC degrading check (not just overall degraded for any reason)
legs_missing: Other checks remain healthy when ONLY orphan events exist. Test plants subjectStore='canonical' with synthetic subjectId NOT in canonical — provenance_references_valid will also flag this as an orphan because the subject isn't in canonical/artifact. Cannot isolate which check is the regression target.
findings.evidence: test lines 211-220 plants subjectStore='canonical' with synthetic subjectId. verify.ts:111 only skips ledger/index subjects — canonical subjects still get the .exists() check. So provenance_references_valid would fail for this synthetic event too.

findings.invariant: STORES-R2-002 — promoted contract methods (importSnapshot×2, importEvent, importReceipt) actually compile-fail when omitted from concrete implementations
legs_asserted: spawned `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0 for each fixture with @ts-expect-error on the class header
legs_missing: A POSITIVE counter-fixture: a fixture WITHOUT @ts-expect-error and WITH the required method should also compile cleanly (proof that adding the method satisfies the contract)
Verification that the fixtures are NOT included in the production tsc -p . build, or that the build excludes test/fixtures/
findings.evidence: test L142-192 spawns tsc on each fixture standalone. The @ts-expect-error matches a real error but doesn't verify what the error is (could be ANY type error matching the directive). A future refactor that breaks the fixture unrelatedly would satisfy @ts-expect-error and pass.

invariants_audited: SURFACE-R2-003 SDK.resolve 5 store-type sanitization + MCP boundary parity
TESTS-R2-001 ReceiptFailedError dirty store + no receipt + citation
summary: Half-invariant test meta-pattern recurs in Wave A3 at lower density than A1/A2. Most consequential: SURFACE-R2-003 added SDK sanitization for all 5 store types but MCP's handler still only special-cases artifact + canonical, and no test exercises ledger/index/receipt URIs through handleTool('cluster_resolve', ...) — same gap shape re-audit-2 flagged. Second-order: two HIGH disjunction-style assertions in SDK.resolve 5-store-type tests allow regressions emitting only one sanitization marker to pass silently. Third: KERNEL-R2-001 inspectCommand oracle test only probes denial at coarse pre-fetch gate; second-stage commandVerb-refined denial path still leaks. Lower-severity: TraceBuilder mutation_orphaned pinned only with negative assertion, receipt_details rule path untested, link_evidence orphan citation missing, Windows symlink test silently returns on EPERM, STORES-R2-005 atomic-ingest test fails to exercise cleanup branch.
